defs: Looking; appearing; as, better or worse liking. See Like, to look.
quotes: Why should he see your faces worse liking than the children which are of your sort?

defs: The state of being pleasing; a suiting. See On liking, below.

The state of being pleased with, or attracted toward, some thing or person; hence, inclination; desire; pleasure; preference; -- often with for, formerly with to; as, it is an amusement I have no liking for.
quotes: If the human intellect hath once taken a liking to any doctrine, . . . it draws everything else into harmony with that doctrine, and to its support.

defs: Appearance; look; figure; state of body as to health or condition.
quotes: I shall think the worse of fat men, as long as I have an eye to make difference of men's liking.
Their young ones are in good liking.
Would he be the degenerate scion of that royal line . . . to be a king on liking and on sufferance?
